page: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m7a.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation with Clang, and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks also ranks in the top tier, while uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the strong category. Single-core performance across stress-ng and Geekbench is consistently strong. For database operations, the instance achieves top-tier results in Redis SET operations and strong performance in Passmark database tests. Large language model inference benchmarks show top-tier speeds for medium model text generation and small model prompt processing.

The absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space or hardware-accelerated parallel processing. However, the high resource density of dedicated physical cores and DDR5 memory provides qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy and memory-intensive general-purpose workloads. This instance is configured for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database hosting, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, delivering a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage with a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong database and CPU-based large language model inference capabilities. This profile makes the instance suitable for multi-threaded workloads, database hosting, software development pipelines, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge features 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 256 GB DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC processor instance with DDR5 memory, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for demanding general-purpose and database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
256.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU, software compilation, and Redis performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
Suited for database hosting, multi-threaded applications, and CPU-based inference
